findings.description: The HTML conversion renders the table with significantly different column widths and row heights, causing the compact single-page PDF table to span multiple pages in the HTML. The overall dense tabular layout is lost.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows a compact table with all 29 rows visible on one page. HTML page 1 shows much taller rows with wrapped text in Owner-Facility and Case Number columns, fitting far fewer rows per page.

findings.description: Table data is preserved but cell content wrapping differs significantly. The Owner-Facility column text is heavily abbreviated/wrapped in HTML compared to the PDF's wider column display.
findings.evidence: PDF shows full facility names in one or two lines. HTML wraps facility names across 3-5 lines due to narrower column width.

findings.description: The title 'DWQ January 2012 Civil Penalty Assessments' has slightly different styling - HTML uses a horizontal rule/line below the title that doesn't appear in the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF: centered title with no underline. HTML: left-aligned title with horizontal rule underneath.

findings.description: The Enforcement Costs column is missing data for several Fuqua Parks entries in both PDF and HTML, which is consistent - no data loss.
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML show blank Enforcement Costs for LV-2011-0326 through LV-2011-0331.

findings.description: The 'Total Cases: 35' footer text is preserved in the HTML conversion.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 shows 'Total Cases: 35' below the table. HTML page 4 shows 'Total Cases: 35' below the table.

findings.description: Reading order is preserved - all rows appear in the same sequence in both PDF and HTML versions.
findings.evidence: Cases start with DV-2012-0003 and end with MV-2012-0002 in both versions.

findings.description: Nested <main> elements in HTML structure. The document has a <main> inside another <main>, which is invalid HTML structure.
findings.evidence: The HTML has <main> as the outer wrapper and another <main> inside it after the page-header div.
